summary.intent: Fix three code review comments from PR #515 in the moltis repo: a slow timeout test, an overly high validation timeout constant, and missing user feedback when a model times out.
summary.outcome: All three issues were addressed — timeout test now runs in ~2s via start_paused, VALIDATION_MAX_TIMEOUTS reduced to 1, and a UI hint added for silent timeouts. Changes committed and pushed.

repo: The moltis repo uses a worktree-based workflow under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/
JS in crates/web/src/assets/js/ uses var throughout, meaning variable declarations are function-scoped and hoisted
Provider setup logic lives in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs, chat logic in crates/chat/src/lib.rs

code.finding: modelTimedOut is set inside an if (selectedModelId) block but is accessible after it due to var hoisting; evaluates as undefined (falsy) when selectedModelId is falsy, which is safe
status element is created after the if (selectedModelId) block closes, so timeout-hint UI must be appended after this line

workflow: start_paused = true can be used in timeout tests to avoid burning real wall-clock time in CI
The 1500ms auto-close delay for status hints is too short to read; needs to be longer when displaying actionable text

friction: Variable scoping confusion: modelTimedOut appeared out-of-scope at the status line until recognizing the codebase uses var (function-scoped) rather than let/const (block-scoped)
Auto-close timing had to be adjusted after initial implementation — 1500ms was too fast to read the hint
